**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Triggering the v5.7 cross-domain engineering hodgepodge and space limitation circuit breaker. Officials slapped this enterprise with the high-tech 'Electronic Device Manufacturing' tag, and its business scope ambitiously includes the R&D and production of 'semiconductor devices, optical spare parts.' However, the probe immediately executed a cruel physical demotion: the company's paid-in capital is 3.38 million, and there are 11 insured employees. The most core flaw lies in its registered address: 'Room 301-6, Third Floor, Huamai International Plaza.' In a single room on the third floor of a commercial plaza, it is absolutely impossible to open a semiconductor wafer dicing or optical lens grinding production line. Skipping these false manufacturing permits, the probe extracted its true survival skills from the latter half of the lengthy business license: 'lighting engineering design and construction, electromechanical engineering construction, residential interior decoration, sports venue facility engineering, cultural relic protection engineering, earthwork engineering.' The truth comes to light: this is fundamentally not a semiconductor or optoelectronic manufacturing plant, but a [General Construction & Lighting Engineering Contractor] cloaked in technology. These 11 employees mainly consist of project managers and salespeople, relying on personal connections to undertake various miscellaneous projects locally, ranging from interior decoration to street lighting, and even excavating earthwork and building playgrounds.
